description: Adventure requires element configurations that emphasize journey, exploration, and escalating physical challenges across varied and distinctive settings.

character_constraints.description: A resourceful, physically capable character who drives forward through obstacles and unknown territory.
An opposing force — may be a villain, a hostile environment, or a rival pursuing the same goal.
A companion or team member who provides complementary skills and shared risk.
A guide who provides knowledge of the terrain, history, or skills needed for the journey.

relationship_constraints.description: Bonds forged through shared danger — the journey tests and deepens alliances.

place_constraints.description: The adventure territory — unfamiliar, dangerous, and varied. Must include at least two distinct environments.
The safe starting point that the protagonist leaves behind, establishing what's at stake.
A point of no return — geographic or situational — that commits the protagonist to the journey.

object_constraints.description: The goal object — treasure, artifact, destination, or person that motivates the journey.
Equipment essential for survival — maps, weapons, vehicles, or specialized gear.

element_rules.description: The protagonist must traverse multiple distinct environments — the journey itself is the story.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least 2 places of type 'special_world' appear across different timeline moments.

element_rules.description: Physical challenges must escalate — each obstacle must be harder than the last.
element_rules.testable_condition: At least one 'loses' transition occurs in the second half of the timeline, raising stakes beyond initial challenges.

element_rules.description: The protagonist must demonstrate resourcefulness — solving problems through skill and ingenuity, not luck.
element_rules.testable_condition: The protagonist has at least 2 'gains' or 'learns' transitions that are preceded by challenge moments.
